title: Operation Sindoor: Government Releases Names of 6 Soldiers Killed in Action for First Time
summary: The Indian government publicly disclosed the names of six soldiers killed in action during Operation Sindoor for the first time. This marks a significant transparency measure regarding casualties in the military operation. The disclosure came through official government channels and was widely reported by major news organisations including Hindustan Times and NDTV.
exam_angle: Operation Sindoor is a key current affairs topic related to India's defence and security operations; official casualty disclosures are significant for civil-military relations and government transparency norms.
key_facts: Number of soldiers named: 6
Operation: Operation Sindoor
First time names were officially made public
Reported on: June 26, 2026

title: India-US Trade Deal: Piyush Goyal Says No Deal Without Competitive Tariff Advantage for India
summary: Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India will not finalise a trade deal with the United States unless India secures a competitive tariff advantage over its rivals in key export sectors. Two-day trade talks between the two sides concluded with India firm on this red line. The US side sought market access concessions while India sought lower tariffs than competitors like China and Vietnam. The Trump administration simultaneously warned that old (higher) tariffs could return if deals are not finalised.
exam_angle: India-US bilateral trade negotiations are directly relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy); key concepts include Most Favoured Nation, competitive tariff advantage, and bilateral trade agreements.
key_facts: India's condition: competitive tariff advantage over rival exporters
Minister involved: Piyush Goyal (Commerce & Industry)
US warning: old tariffs may return if deals not struck
Context: ongoing India-US Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) negotiations

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Scotch Whisky and Gin Tariffs to Reduce from July 2026
summary: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement begins its tariff reduction phase from July 2026, with Scotch whisky and gin becoming cheaper for Indian consumers. The FTA, signed earlier, provides for phased reduction of duties on UK alcoholic beverages and other goods entering India, while Indian textiles and pharmaceuticals benefit from lower UK tariffs. This is one of the most significant bilateral trade deals India has concluded in recent years.
exam_angle: The India-UK FTA is a landmark trade agreement; understanding its provisions, especially phased tariff concessions and sensitive sectors, is important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy).
key_facts: Agreement: India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA)
Effective for tariff reductions: July 2026
Key UK export benefiting: Scotch whisky, gin
India gains: lower tariffs on textiles, pharmaceuticals in UK market

title: US Assures India that AI Technology Access Once Granted Will Not Be Revoked: IT Secretary S. Krishnan
summary: India's IT Secretary S. Krishnan confirmed that the United States has given assurances that technology access — particularly for artificial intelligence — once provided to India will not be cut off. The assurance came during bilateral technology discussions and follows India raising concerns about suspended access to Anthropic AI models. The US stated it is taking a 'measured approach' on the Anthropic issue and is in active talks with India.
exam_angle: India-US technology partnership, AI access, and technology transfer issues are relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech); connects to India's AI policy goals under IndiaAI Mission.
key_facts: Official: IT Secretary S. Krishnan
Assurance: AI technology access once granted will not be revoked
Issue flagged by India: suspended access to Anthropic AI models
Context: India-US bilateral technology cooperation talks

title: White House Asks OpenAI to Limit Early Release of GPT-5.6 to Small Group
summary: The White House directed OpenAI to limit the early release of its latest large language model, GPT-5.6, to a small, controlled group of users, citing concerns about AI safety and governance. This represents a significant instance of executive intervention in commercial AI deployment in the United States. The move is part of broader US government efforts to regulate the pace of advanced AI model releases.
exam_angle: US government regulation of AI models is relevant to global AI governance debates; connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) topics on AI regulation, safety, and international norms being discussed at forums India participates in.
key_facts: Directive from: White House (Trump administration)
Company: OpenAI
Model: GPT-5.6
Action: limit early access to a small controlled group

title: Venezuela Earthquakes: Death Toll Rises to 235, International Rescue Effort Underway
summary: A series of powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, with the death toll rising to 235 as rescue operations continued. International aid and rescue teams joined local efforts to search through collapsed buildings. Satellite imagery showed widespread destruction across affected regions. Venezuela's Vice President Delcy Rodriguez thanked PM Modi and India for their support.
exam_angle: Major natural disasters, international humanitarian response, and India's disaster diplomacy are relevant for GS Paper 1 (Geography) and GS Paper 2 (IR); also tests knowledge of Venezuela's geographic location and tectonic setting.
key_facts: Country affected: Venezuela
Death toll: 235 (as of June 26, 2026)
India's response: PM Modi expressed support; Venezuela thanked India
Tremors reportedly felt as far as 14,000 km away

title: India Withdraws Commercial LPG Supply Restrictions Amid Hopes of US-Iran Nuclear Deal
summary: India has withdrawn restrictions it had placed on commercial LPG supplies, a move linked to growing optimism around a potential US-Iran nuclear agreement that could ease global energy supply constraints. The lifting of restrictions is expected to benefit domestic LPG consumers and commercial users. The development reflects India's energy security strategy and its sensitivity to geopolitical shifts in West Asia.
exam_angle: India's energy security policy, LPG supply management, and the impact of geopolitical developments on India's hydrocarbon imports are key topics for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: Action: India withdraws commercial LPG supply restrictions
Trigger: hopes of US-Iran nuclear deal easing global energy supply
Commodity: Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG)
Relevance: India's energy security and West Asia geopolitics

title: CBI Raids Over 80 Locations Across India, Busts 'Digital Arrest' Fraud Network Using Fake Supreme Court Website
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) conducted searches at over 80 locations across India as part of a major crackdown on 'digital arrest' scams. The fraudsters operated an elaborate network that used a fake Supreme Court website to intimidate victims into paying money under the threat of fake digital arrest warrants. The operation targeted multiple states and resulted in recovery of digital evidence and arrests.
exam_angle: CBI's jurisdiction, cybercrime enforcement, and the growing menace of digital arrest scams (flagged by PM Modi in Mann Ki Baat) are relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security).
key_facts: Agency: Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI)
Locations raided: 80+, across multiple states
Fraud type: 'Digital Arrest' scam
Method: fake Supreme Court website used to intimidate victims

title: Drone Strike Hits Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz as West Asia Ceasefire Frays
summary: A drone strike targeted a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about the fragility of the ceasefire in West Asia. The incident coincided with Iran proposing a $40 billion plan to levy charges on ships transiting through Hormuz, adding to regional maritime security tensions. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical global oil chokepoint through which a significant share of India's crude oil imports pass.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is the world's most important oil chokepoint; disruptions directly affect India's energy security; relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Internal Security).
key_facts: Location: Strait of Hormuz
Incident: drone strike on cargo ship
Iran proposal: $40 billion plan to charge ships transiting Hormuz
Significance: ~20% of global oil trade passes through Hormuz; critical for India's energy imports

title: Bangladesh Acting PM Tarique Rahman Meets Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing
summary: Bangladesh's acting Prime Minister Tarique Rahman met Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, signalling a strengthening of Dhaka-Beijing ties following the political changes in Bangladesh. The meeting is significant from India's strategic perspective given Bangladesh's geographic importance as a neighbouring country and China's growing influence in South Asia. Discussions are believed to have covered economic cooperation and infrastructure investment.
exam_angle: China's expanding footprint in India's neighbourhood (Bangladesh) is directly relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) — India's neighbourhood policy, China-Bangladesh relations, and the strategic implications for India.
key_facts: Leaders: Tarique Rahman (Bangladesh acting PM) and Xi Jinping (Chinese President)
Meeting location: Beijing
Significance: signals deepening Bangladesh-China ties post-Hasina government
India angle: strategic concern given Bangladesh's role in India's neighbourhood policy

title: Goldman Sachs Raises India's FY27 GDP Growth Forecast to 6.5%, Projects Lower Inflation
summary: Global investment bank Goldman Sachs revised upward its forecast for India's GDP growth in FY2026-27 to 6.5%, citing improved macroeconomic conditions and policy stability. The bank also projected inflation to remain at lower levels, supporting consumer demand and investment. This follows similar upgrades by other multilateral agencies reflecting confidence in India's growth trajectory.
exam_angle: GDP growth forecasts by global agencies are standard current affairs for GS Paper 3 (Economy); key for understanding India's macroeconomic position and comparison with IMF/World Bank projections.
key_facts: Agency: Goldman Sachs
India FY27 GDP growth forecast: 6.5%
Outlook: inflation projected at lower levels
Significance: reflects positive macroeconomic sentiment on India

title: Foxconn Singapore Acquires Stake in India Subsidiary for $37.2 Million
summary: Foxconn Singapore has acquired a stake in its India subsidiary for $37.2 million, reinforcing the Taiwan-based electronics giant's commitment to expanding manufacturing in India. Foxconn is a key supplier for Apple and other global tech companies, and its India investments are central to the government's push to position India as a global electronics manufacturing hub under the PLI scheme.
exam_angle: Foxconn's investment connects to India's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for electronics, Apple's India manufacturing push, and Make in India — all important GS Paper 3 topics.
key_facts: Company: Foxconn Singapore
Transaction: acquisition of stake in India subsidiary
Value: $37.2 million
Significance: supports India's electronics manufacturing under PLI scheme

title: UK Arrests Indian Merchant Navy Captain Over Russian Crude Shipment in Violation of Sanctions
summary: United Kingdom authorities arrested an Indian merchant navy captain for transporting Russian crude oil in alleged violation of Western sanctions imposed following Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The arrest highlights the risks faced by Indian shipping interests navigating the complex sanctions regime around Russian energy. India has been a major buyer of discounted Russian crude since 2022.
exam_angle: India's Russian crude imports, Western sanctions on Russia, and the legal risks for Indian shipping are directly relevant to GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy); also touches on energy security policy.
key_facts: Action: UK arrested an Indian merchant navy captain
Charge: breaching Western sanctions by transporting Russian crude oil
Context: India has been the largest buyer of Russian crude post-2022 Ukraine war sanctions
Relevance: impact on India's shipping industry and energy trade diplomacy

title: Passport as Citizenship Proof Row: Shashi Tharoor Questions Government Clarification
summary: A controversy erupted over whether an Indian passport constitutes proof of citizenship, after the government issued a clarification that passports are not citizenship documents per se. MP Shashi Tharoor publicly questioned the government's position, pointing out that government documents themselves describe passports as citizenship proof. The debate has constitutional implications around the Citizenship Act and the proposed National Register of Citizens.
exam_angle: The legal distinction between a passport (travel document) and proof of citizenship is important for Polity; connects to Citizenship Act 1955, NRC debate, and Articles 5-11 of the Constitution.
key_facts: Issue: whether Indian passport constitutes citizenship proof
Government position: passport is a travel document, not citizenship certificate
Critic: MP Shashi Tharoor
Relevant law: Citizenship Act, 1955; Articles 5-11 of the Constitution

title: Ram Temple Donation Row: All 8 Accused Arrested, Champat Rai Steps Down as Trust Official
summary: All eight accused named in the FIR related to alleged theft of donations at the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple in Ayodhya have been arrested. Champat Rai, a key official of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, stepped down amid the donation controversy. UP Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath warned against 'playing with faith' and announced that a Special Investigation Team (SIT) will probe the matter.
exam_angle: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is a statutory trust established under Supreme Court directions; accountability of religious trusts and the role of state in investigating such matters is relevant to GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance).
key_facts: Location: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Temple, Ayodhya
Accused arrested: 8
Official resigned: Champat Rai (General Secretary, Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust)
Investigation: SIT to be set up by UP government

title: Europe's Record Heatwave 'Virtually Impossible' Without Climate Change, Scientists Confirm
summary: Climate scientists confirmed that the record-shattering heatwave currently affecting Europe would have been 'virtually impossible' without human-induced climate change. Paris and other European cities imposed restrictions on alcohol consumption and public gatherings as temperatures soared. The findings, published through the World Weather Attribution methodology, reinforce the scientific consensus on climate change's role in amplifying extreme weather events.
exam_angle: Attribution science linking extreme weather events to climate change is key for GS Paper 3 (Environment); also relevant to UNFCCC, Paris Agreement commitments, and climate justice discussions.
key_facts: Event: Record European heatwave (June 2026)
Scientific finding: 'virtually impossible' without climate change
Methodology: World Weather Attribution
Response: Paris restricted alcohol sales; heat action plans activated across Europe

title: CBSE Clarifies: Students in Classes 7–9 with Foreign Language Combination May Continue Until Class 10
summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) clarified that students currently enrolled in Classes 7 to 9 who are studying a foreign language as part of their subject combination will be permitted to continue with that combination until Class 10. This came after a new policy restricted certain language combinations in middle school. The clarification provides relief to existing students mid-course.
exam_angle: CBSE policy changes are relevant to GS Paper 2 (Governance/Education) and reflect implementation of NEP 2020's language policy; important for aspirants tracking education sector developments.
key_facts: Board: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
Ruling: students in Classes 7–9 with foreign language can continue until Class 10
Context: new restrictions on language combinations in middle school
Policy link: NEP 2020's three-language formula

title: Gilgit-Baltistan's Sectarian Divide: Strategic and Governance Dimensions
summary: An analysis piece highlights how the sectarian divide in Gilgit-Baltistan (GB) — a region under Pakistani occupation — was engineered through deliberate demographic and governance policies rather than being organic. GB has strategic importance as it lies along the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) and borders India's Ladakh region. India does not recognise Pakistani jurisdiction over GB, claiming it as part of Jammu & Kashmir.
exam_angle: Gilgit-Baltistan is strategically critical — it is the gateway to CPEC, borders Ladakh, and is part of India's territorial claims on J&K; essential for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security).
key_facts: Region: Gilgit-Baltistan (under Pakistan's occupation)
India's position: claims GB as part of J&K; does not recognise Pakistani jurisdiction
Strategic link: gateway to China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC)
Borders: India's Ladakh UT to the east
